instruction:
Create a complete Node-Watch FastAPI backend. Build /root/sovereign/shared/results/node-watch-api/ with these files:

1. main.py - FastAPI app with endpoints:
   - POST /api/v1/metrics - receive/store metric payloads in SQLite
   - GET /api/v1/servers - list all servers with last-seen timestamps
   - GET /api/v1/servers/{server_id}/metrics?hours=24 - return time-series metrics
   - GET /api/v1/servers/{server_id}/status - health status (healthy/warning/critical based on thresholds)
   - POST /api/v1/alerts/configure - set alert thresholds (store in DB)
   - GET /health - health check
   - Simple API key auth via X-API-Key header

2. database.py - SQLite schema + operations:
   - metrics table: server_id, timestamp, cpu_percent, memory_percent, disk_percent, load_avg, network_sent, network_recv, processes_json
   - servers table: server_id, first_seen, last_seen, hostname
   - alerts table: server_id, metric_name, threshold, email

3. requirements.txt - FastAPI, uvicorn, sqlite3 (built-in)

4. Dockerfile - Multi-stage build for production deployment

5. docker-compose.yml - Service definition for port 9081

Status logic:
- healthy: CPU <80%, RAM <85%, disk <90%, last_seen <5min
- warning: One metric exceeds threshold OR last_seen 5-15min  
- critical: Multiple metrics exceed OR last_seen >15min

Handle all errors gracefully. Auto-create SQLite DB on startup. Include proper logging. No authentication for GET endpoints (read-only). Production-ready code.
